// Towards Ultra-Long-Horizon Agentic Science //

These researchers finally got long-horizon research agents to hold together for a full day.

Worth reading if you care about how autonomous research agents actually scale past one session.

A team from SJTU ran ML-Master 2.0 on https://t.co/advh1kYUoS

Video of my opening keynote for the 2026 World Modeling Workshop at Mila, Quebec AI Institute: simple but powerful ways of using world models and their latent space (sorry for sound problem 0:20-0:43). Details and references in: The Neural World Model Boom https://t.co/bRz8PFNt0P https://t.co/7qFZXcuM7o

As a general rule, people overestimate China's chipmaking prowess and underestimate their everything-else-making prowess

Chips are the top tier of complexity which gives the US and its allies an edge + makes export controls important

Elsewhere we are complacent (e.g. robotics)

this is the year of 3D World models 🔥 

> Lyra 2.0 by NVIDIA: image → 3D world with Gaussians, 14B params, built on WAN-14B 

> HY-World 2.0 by Tencent: text/image/video 3D  → editable world (meshes + Gaussians) drop-in to Blender/Unity/Unreal

weights on the next one ➡️ https://t.co/MTSdHmRE2P

And this is a very generous definition of notable.

If we are talking frontier models, only the US and China that are even in the race. And that obscures the fact that the Big Three US labs really do seem to be holding a durable lead, albeit one measured in months, not years.

Third-party auditing is indeed the right middle path in AI policy, between excessive deference to industry on one end and excessive concentration of power in government on the other https://t.co/Nv8CwF4YCZ

Great paper on self-improving agents. 

Why? 

We need to think more deeply about AI agent system design.

The protocol specifies a framework for proposing, assessing, and committing improvements with auditable lineage and rollback.

Visual below (courtesy of my research agent). https://t.co/rOKR4HRbrF
but what if we made an LLM from scratch?

I am not convinced that we should be comfortable calling "problem solving" or "judgement" or whatever as skills that are impossible for AI to do well. Like any other skill, there are humans who are really good at it, but that doesn't mean that AIs don't do good judgement, etc.

The OpenAI Academy is a learning platform that helps people - more than 3M so far - build practical AI skills through live events, training resources, expert content, and industry-specific communities.

This week, our teams hosted events from Warsaw, Poland to Abilene, Texas to https://t.co/Yrh7F2SBXt

Opus 4.6 crossed a threshold for general-purpose knowledge work agents where several problems are just "solved" - deep research, personal admin, etc.

Feels similar to Claude 3.7 moment for code. Enabled agentic coding to gain widespread adoption

This gap will get filled

An obvious way to release Mythos class models with uncertain autonomous ability is to make them only available on the website, like Gemini Deep Think or ChatGPT Pro.

Minimal risk of being used for autonomous hacking, but accessible to people who have hard problems to solve.

Elon just mapped out AGI.

Grok 4.4 → 1T params, early May
Grok 4.5 → 1.5T params, late May
Grok 5 → AGI

That's two model releases standing between us and AGI according to Elon 🤯 https://t.co/7Uly7IDyhV

One of my hard-earned, multiply-repeated life lessons is learning that someone “working a field all their life” is actually a handicap in many situations, increasingly with AI. Knowing which situations is the hardest lesson of all.
Beamer is a clear indication of a postdoc candidate

The computer is for you https://t.co/xYIJfN5FAS

Qwen 3.6 is here, and open-source! Run it locally with improved agentic coding capabilities.

Try it with Claude Code: 
ollama launch claude --model qwen3.6 

Try it with OpenClaw: 
ollama launch openclaw --model qwen3.6

Run it: 
ollama run qwen3.6

Gemini CLI’s new Subagents feature is a game-changer for terminal productivity. It turns your primary agent into a strategic manager that delegates sub-tasks to specialized experts.

👉 Subagents keep the main window fast by handling high-volume tool calls in isolation
👉 Run https://t.co/TfLwAkux0a

New Eval mode: Battles in Direct

We sample two random anonymous models during Direct chats - enabling pairwise comparison beyond turn 1.

Why this matters:
• Evaluates under longer context + multi-turn dependency
• Captures failure modes: drift, consistency, recovery
• Closer https://t.co/knpvTsxrKp

Introducing Tab Tab Tab, our new prompt auto complete engine in @GoogleAIStudio's vibe coding experience. 

Now when you show up with your fuzzy ideas, you can rely on Gemini to fill in the blanks : ) https://t.co/VHTt6ztOXy

Human biological limits, like our tiny working memory and shallow calculation depth, are actually a feature. They force us to abstract, compress, intuit. If we had infinite resources, we would never have needed intelligence.

I am very proud of our team for releasing EDINET-Bench, and it is fantastic to see a Japanese financial dataset recognized at #ICLR2026 this week. We need more diverse, non-English datasets to evaluate models in the real world.

https://t.co/Q9y4xwWqgM https://t.co/Bh6LgNOMG4

YT Podcast → LLM Artifact

This is now my favorite way to consume podcasts. 

Knowledge artifacts generated by agents.

The agent (Opus 4.7) spots important insights, does deep analysis, and generates thought-provoking observations that really get me curious to research further. https://t.co/RvLFJQE3Ms

Holy smokes... Hermes Agent just hit 100K GitHub stars.

It's an AI Agent like OpenClaw but with real self-improving skills and much better memory

100K in under 2 months.

It's coming for OpenClaw.🤯 https://t.co/Wjh6aTUY54

OMG.  Let’s get one thing straight. Claude doesn’t get anxious. 

It mimics people who get anxious.

Those two things are NOT the same.

My head is shaking so much I need medical attention.

The companies that will shape AI are the ones that understand every layer, from chips to applications.

Our CEO Jensen Huang joined @Cadence CEO @OfficialADevgan at #CadenceLIVE on why the engineers and companies building the future need to think across the entire stack. https://t.co/vLgsDVI92N
codex is becoming the universal app for developers:
Damn this paper strikes again https://t.co/2tYtWkyjW0

300+ humanoid robots just raced humans in Beijing.

Last year's winner: 2 hrs 40 min
This year's winner: 50 min 26 sec

3x faster. In one year.

The winning robot beat the human world record by more than 6 minutes. Autonomously. 🤯

It's happening.
https://t.co/qEBYErAm9g

I think Artificial Analysis does a good job overall and provides transparency in benchmarking, but GDPval-AA is not a good benchmark and needs to stop being reported. It is Gemini 3.1 judging other models on the output of the public questions from GDPval, which tells us nothing.

This Claude Design prompt literally created an escape game🤯

PROMPT: "Create an escape game using Three.js."
https://t.co/MsKL23taID

when people ask what my "field" is, I struggle to answer. I spend my time in the intersections - of imaging, CV, AI, statistics - and so I’ve seldom felt like an "insider" in any one of them

it reminds me of Max Weber who famously said “I'm not a donkey; I don’t have a field”

What gets missed with AI productivity gains is that by and large, most roles will continue to be as sophisticated as the tools allow. 

This is why also thinking through “today’s jobs will be replaced with AI” is a fallacy. Everyone thinks the market is static, but it’s not.

As

I had early access to o1-preview. I still remember my shock after reading responses to some of my prompts! It was the second turning point for me after ChatGPT 3.5. And now here we are, it's the big payoff day for the big bet…
Claude’s “emotion” vectors are “emotionlike” in roughly the same sense that counterfeit “money” is “moneylike”.

What a week in SF for Gemma! 💎

- Gemma 4 SF Meetup, with Unsloth, MLX, VLLM, NousResearch, and Cactus Compute
- Hackathon at Y Combinator with Cactus Compute
- Ollama Gemma 4 Meetup, with SGLang
- NVIDIA Developer Meetup

I had lots of fun in this visit, until the next time! https://t.co/mmMWXj888Y

When we succeed at making the most performant full stack from Tensor to hardware for both major GPUs in 25k lines, a lot of people are going to have to ask what the other 10M lines were doing. Creating jobs?

The forgotten middle.

Making AI work for the millions of non Fortune 500 companies who need to make everyday decisions.

They also don’t want to be dependent on a handful of proprietary providers, and want more control to own and shape. 

Completely agree with this read.

Human cognitive friction has long been acting as a regularizer for a lot of digital infrastructure. It made software APIs less terrible and codebases less complex.

Now LLM disintermediation is causing this effect to fade, which in turn will cause runaway software complexity.
I like the little multiple choice follow-up question things in the Claude app (maybe has happened in ChatGPT / Codex sometimes also, I forget)

A real issue with the current state of our knowledge on the work implications of AI is that there was a genuine discontinuity in AI ability with the rise of practical agentic systems in 2026.

We were starting to get a picture of the impact of chatbots, no real data on agents.

Neither company should caricature the other. I’ve said multiple times that I’m glad Anthropic exists. It would be very bad if only one company was making frontier AI, whether it’s OpenAI, Anthropic, Google or anyone else..

Huawei proposes HiFloat4 FP4 (HiF4) format for Huawei Ascend NPUs and achieves performance comparable to BF16 baselines, while delivering substantial improvements in computational efficiency. https://t.co/0jOom6SSCu
